statute: Louisiana Revised Statutes § 37:2150 et seq. (LSLBC); Home Improvement Contractors Act R.S. § 37:2167
license_label: Louisiana State Licensing Board for Contractors (LSLBC) License Number
license_notes: Commercial license required for projects over $50,000. Residential license for projects over $75,000. Home improvement contractors must register when project is $7,500-$75,000. Subcontractors need license for work over $7,500.

mandatory_clauses.label: LSLBC license disclosure
Louisiana lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS (STATEMENT OF CLAIM AND PRIVILEGE)

Under Louisiana law (R.S. § 9:4801 et seq.), contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who are not paid may file a claim and privilege (lien) against your property. To protect your interests, obtain lien waivers and releases from all contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Unlicensed = misdemeanor. Fine up to 10% of contract value. Contract becomes null and void. Unlicensed contractors can only recover minimum value of work under lien law.
notes: Louisiana has tiered licensing: residential license for >$75,000; commercial for >$50,000; home improvement registration for $7,500-$75,000. Work under $7,500 exempt. Contractor must register with Secretary of State. Reciprocity with GA, TN, MS, NC. Louisiana lien law is unique — uses 'Statement of Claim and Privilege' rather than typical mechanics lien.
